observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to conduct immediate reconciliation of the individual balances of the Cash-in Local Treasury account with the pertinent Cashbook/s and to make the necessary adjustments thereon.
Further, direct the Municipal Accountant to record the reconciling items in the books of accounts based on the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) prepared. Moreover, for the Municipal Accountant to also furnish the Municipal Treasurer copies of the BRS for the recording of reconciling items, if any, pertaining to his records.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management:
Revert to the Cash-in Bank, Current Account, of the General Fund the balance of the Time Deposits upon its maturity, after deducting the balance of the Share from National Wealth which shall be transferred to a Trust Fund account in the General Fund, in order that the same may be appropriated and used in the implementation of various PPAs that will benefit the constituents;
Further, the Municipal Accountant shall certify that the funds intended to be placed in a time deposit are essentially idle after regular and recurring operating expenses within the context of the LGU’s cash operating cycle; and
Henceforth, secure prior authority from the SB and the approval of the Local Chief Executive before placement of idle funds under the General Fund in HYSA or Time Deposit accounts.

We reiterated the following previous year’s recommendations:
The MGSO to: (a) Render Road Map; and, (b) Keep a complete and updated Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPCs) for all roads and its components;
The Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the MGSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and,
The Municipal Accountant to: (a) Prepare the JEV to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components; (b) Prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Cards (LRNLCs); (c) Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and (d) Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to reconcile the discrepancies noted between the accounting records and the RPCPPE and effect the necessary adjustments to come up with the correct balances.
Henceforth, require both officials to maintain and regularly update the PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards to facilitate reconciliation of the account balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare an aging of accounts receivables, make the final reconciliation and accounting of these account balances and prepare the DVs together with the supporting schedule for the Municipal Treasurer to issue a check payable either to the Source Agency or direct to the National Treasury.
We further recommended that remaining balances which are considered as special purpose funds should be retained and fund balances corresponding to original releases such as financial assistance or subsidy, as well as funds which the disposition of the unused balance is not specified, maybe reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the Municipality for future utilization thereof subject to the usual accounting, auditing and budgeting laws and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant continue the timely submission of the year-end financial statements, together with its supporting schedules, to enable the timely verification thereof by the Auditor.

We recommended that the Municipality adhere to the mandatory posting on the GPPB Online Portal of information relative to Emergency Procurements under the Bayanihan Act, to ensure that accountability and transparency in the procurement is upheld.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the BAC, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, submit the required Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) to the GPPB on a semestral basis pursuant to Section 12.2 of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and GPPB Circular 03-2015 dated March 20, 2015.

We recommended that management:
Ensure that every Purchase Order be duly filled-in with all the data needed as guide for the suppliers in complying with the terms and conditions of the contract. The delivery term and date of receipt of PO by the supplier as well as the actual date of the receipt and inspection of the items purchased by the concerned personnel/official in the municipality. The data is required in order to have sufficient basis to demand from the supplier liquidated damages or penalty of 1/10 of 1% of the cost of undelivered items per day of delay, in case of the latter’s failure to meet the contract time/period.
Require the Municipal Accountant and Officials concerned to fill-up the relevant and required information on the Purchase Order and Inspection and Acceptance Report. The document must be signed and approved only when the required data are complete to ensure that the supplier will deliver the goods in accordance with the terms and conditions of the contract.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management require the Municipal Property Officer to immediately submit the Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumption for CY 2021. Henceforth, submit the report within ten days after the end of the month to enable the Auditor to conduct prompt verification thereof.

We recommend that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate submission of the lacking documents enumerated in Annex A hereof, otherwise, the non-submission of the required supporting documents shall warrant the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance in audit; and
Henceforth, enjoined the Municipal to process and pay only those disbursement vouchers supported with complete documentations in full compliance with Section 4, Paragraph 6 of P.D. No. 1445 and Section 9.1.3.1 of COA Circular 2012-001.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to release the retention money for infrastructure projects in accordance to Section 6.2 of the “Annex E” of the IRR of RA 9184.
Henceforth, compel all the contractors of the infrastructure projects of the Municipal Government  whose defects liability period had not yet expired and whose retention money were released prior to the lapse of the one year period after completion or was not deducted to the first and final billing of 100% completed projects, as the case maybe, without a valid substitute equivalent to the retention money as prescribed in the IRR of RA 9184, to post the required financial instrument that is valid until the end of the defects liability of the projects, otherwise, demand that they refund or pay the retention money for the projects.
Further, to instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to remind the bidders for the contracts to be bidded by the Municipal Government of the requirement of retention money on projects as provided in the above cited provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that Management must ensure the following:
That the surety bond issued by contractors as performance bonds on infrastructure projects shall contain the specific dates of coverage, specifically the date starting from the effectivity of the bond up to the time of completion of the project and up to one year from completion, including the expiration date thereof. Henceforth, surety bonds posted by contractors with inconsistent or unclear stipulations relative to the validity period are not accepted as Performance Bond.
That projects with approved contract time extensions are supported with evidence that the validity of the performance security is correspondingly extended pursuant to Section 11.5 of Annex “E” of the IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Management require the concerned contractors to post extension of the validity of the respective performance bonds to secure performance of the contractors’ obligations under the project contract and to adhere to the rules and regulation embodied under the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the LCE provide justification why the mandatory requirement for a CPES is not observed.  Henceforth, we enjoin LCE to revisit the provisions as provided under Section 13 of the Annex “E” of the R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR on the evaluation of contractor’s performance.  Further, that concerned department representatives be convened to discuss on the subject matter to ascertain commitment/s in terms of agency resources towards conformance to the mandatory requirement for a CPES and its necessary impact to the operations and structure of the LGU.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management, through the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) facilitate the full  establishment of the GAD database in order to have a basis for planning and programming PPAs and formulating policies that are gender-responsive and to be able to undertake thorough gender analysis based on relevant data to properly identify existing gender issues and ensure that the approved PPAs to be incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget are gender responsive.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person continue to develop and implement GAD related programs/projects/activities that advocate women empowerment, promote gender equality and improve the welfare of women, and ensure that planned GAD PPAs are all timely implemented based on its approved GAD Plan and Budget.
We recommended that the Local DRRM Office be institutionalized and organized according to law by assigning full-time permanent employees thereat to maximize its efficiency and effectiveness.

We recommended to the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management (LDRRM) Officer to optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF of the LGU in order to strengthen its disaster preparedness and avoid potential damages during disasters.
Likewise, to address the gaps in DRRM planning, implementation and monitoring of the programs, projects and activities, we recommend close coordination among LGU officials and other DRRM officer to ensure effective formulation and implementation of the DRR Projects as listed in the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that the municipality strive to improve its efficiency on the utilization of the 20 per cent EDF and to fully implement the priority PPAs, in order to attain the desired outcomes on the socio-economic development that will benefit the intended beneficiaries.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Officer to hasten, monitor and prioritize the implementation and completion of the remaining Social and Economic Development Projects under the 20 per cent EDF.

We recommended that Management prepare and submit the reports and outputs enumerated in DILG MC No. 2012-120 by the end of current year, copy furnished the Audit Team, for the proper and effective implementation of LCPC programs, projects and activities.
We recommended that Management direct the members of the LCPC and MSWDO to closely monitor and implement the PPAs for LCPC to further maximize the utilization of the LCPC fund.

We reiterated the following recommendations:

The MGSO to: (a) Render Road Map; and, (b) Keep a complete and updated Local Road Network Property Card (LRNPCs) for all roads and its components;

The Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the MGSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; and, 

The Municipal Accountant to: (a) Prepare the JEV to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components; (b) Prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Cards (LRNLCs); (c) Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and (d) Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Revert to the Cash-in Bank, Current Account, of the General Fund the balance of the Time Deposits upon its maturity, after deducting the balance of the Share from National Wealth which shall be transferred to a Trust Fund account in the General Fund, in order that the same may be appropriated and used in the implementation of various PPAs that will benefit the constituents;
Utilize the proceeds from the Share from National Wealth strictly in accordance with its purpose pursuant to Section 294 of RA 7160; and
Henceforth, secure prior authority from the SB and the approval of the Local Chief Executive before placement of idle funds under the General Fund in HYSA or Time Deposit accounts.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to review and verify the dormant receivables of the LGU and issue demand letters to recipients/grantees.  If the accounts remained uncollected despite the issuance of at least two demand letters, request for authority to write off the doubtful accounts, in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivables to ensure that these are timely collected when the accounts become due and demandable.

We recommended for the LCE require the Municipal Health Officer and the MGSO to submit the required utilization reports to the Municipal Accountant in order that the Drugs and Medicines that were already distributed can be dropped from the books of accounts.
Henceforth, the AOs shall ensure that the required utilization reports are submitted promptly to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and the MGSO to reconcile the discrepancies noted between the General Ledger and the RPCPPE and effect the necessary adjustments to come up with the correct balances.
Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant and Property Officer to maintain and regularly update the PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, for internal control purposes.

We recommended that Management require concerned officials to submit copies of Contracts/POs to the Auditor, with the required supporting documents attached thereto, within five days after perfection thereof, in compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that Management:
Require the MPDO and the MEO to fast track the implementation of the various PPAs in order to achieve the desired outcomes which are essential in the attainment of the LGU’s goals and objectives in improving the quality of life of its constituents;
b. Maximize the utilization of funds allocated for the implementation of various PPAs during the year that are envisioned to help in the attainment of social, economic and environmental development; and
c. Adopt strategies, despite personnel limitations, so that planned projects by the LGU may be timely implemented and completed within the stipulated time frames.

We recommended that the LDRRM Officer optimize the utilization of the LDRRMF in order to strengthen its disaster preparedness and avoid potential damages during disasters.
Likewise, to address the gaps in DRRM planning, implementation, and monitoring of the programs, projects, and activities, we recommend close coordination among LGU officials and the DRRM Officer to ensure effective formulation and implementation of the DRRM Projects as listed in the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and DRRM Officer to prepare the Reports on Utilization of Cash Donations and the Monthly Report on the Receipt and Utilization of the DRRMF and submit to the Office of the Civil Defense, copy furnished the COA Auditor. Henceforth, comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines as prescribed in keeping with the Circular.
We recommended that the LCE require the SWM Committee to comply with the instructions of the EMB and revise the SWM Plan to comply with requirements of the Ecological SWM Act of 2000.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant continue the timely submission of the year-end FS, together with its supporting schedules and its preparation in conformity with IPSAS.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the Quarterly FS for the third and fourth quarters.  Henceforth, he should prepare and submit the Monthly Trial Balance and Quarterly FS with complete Schedules and Annexes within the prescribed timeframe in accordance with existing rules and regulations;
Take appropriate immediate remedial measures, such as augmenting the MAO with personnel from other departments, especially during report preparation periods and/or allowing them to render overtime, subject to the availability of funds, in order to comply with the timely preparation and submission of mandatory financial reports; and
Allow qualified plantilla personnel of the MAO to attend trainings or seminars relative to their job descriptions to equip them with the required knowledge and skills in order to improve their performance.

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation for the LCE to direct the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Assessor, and other concerned Department Heads to prepare and submit to the Sangguniang Bayan for approval the proposed adjustment on tax rates to increase its revenue collections.

We recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan review, revise and amend the existing Local Revenue Code of the Municipality enacted in 2003 and request the LCE to require the concerned Department Heads to prepare proposed adjustments on tax rates as prescribed under the Code. 
We also recommended for the LCE to direct the Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Assessor and other concerned Department Heads to prepare and submit to the Sangguniang Bayan for approval the proposed adjustment on tax rates in an effort to increase/maximize its revenue collections which will result in higher income for the LGU to augment the budget provision for the implementation of the Municipality’s projects for public services.
